summary: Established the FOUNDATION-005 test baseline with root Vitest/fast-check coverage, Storybook build coverage, and Playwright Chromium e2e coverage for the existing web shell. The test_engineer thread implemented the baseline; the lead_orchestrator performed scoped integration fixes so the new files are covered by the existing format/lint/typecheck gates and local test outputs do not dirty the workspace.

tests_run.command: pnpm add -Dw react@19.2.7 react-dom@19.2.7 vite@8.1.0 vitest@4.1.9 fast-check @fast-check/vitest @playwright/test storybook @storybook/react-vite
tests_run.result: Initial dependency add was blocked by pnpm 11 build approval for esbuild: ERR_PNPM_IGNORED_BUILDS.

tests_run.command: pnpm add -Dw --allow-build=esbuild react@19.2.7 react-dom@19.2.7 vite@8.1.0 vitest@4.1.9 fast-check @fast-check/vitest @playwright/test storybook @storybook/react-vite
tests_run.result: Root devDependencies and pnpm-lock.yaml updated; pnpm-workspace.yaml records allowBuilds.esbuild.
Scope all 10 workspace projects; lockfile up to date; esbuild postinstall completed under allowBuilds.
First lead verification failed because root tsconfig.json did not include the new tests, Storybook files, and Playwright/Vitest configs for ESLint project service.
Prettier, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture check passed after tsconfig/package script coverage fixes.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic hello matched Node and worker-compatible hashes 61e0ba1c; root Vitest passed 1 file/2 tests; web Vitest passed 1 file/3 tests.
Storybook v10.4.6 build completed successfully to .tmp/storybook-static and the script removed the output directory afterwards. Vite emitted a chunk size warning only.
Playwright Chromium ran 1 test and passed. Vite emitted the existing dynamic import analysis warning for apps/web/src/worker/hello-simulation-adapter.mjs.

decisions: Added root Vitest config and tests rather than moving existing app tests, preserving the F004 app shell and adding a separate F005 baseline.
Used fast-check directly in Vitest to create a property baseline without changing simulation or client implementation code.
Added Storybook files under .storybook and a single WebClientShell story that reuses the existing read-model shell.
Added Playwright config with Chromium project and a Vite dev server for the existing web shell.

Kept package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, pnpm-workspace.yaml, tsconfig.json, and .gitignore in F005 via explicit lead scope reconciliation because the required root commands, pnpm build approval, ESLint project-service coverage, and test output hygiene depend on them.
risks: Playwright uses fixed local port 4173; a conflicting process on that port can fail e2e.
Vite still warns that the existing dynamic import in apps/web/src/worker/hello-simulation-adapter.mjs cannot be statically analyzed. It is pre-existing behavior and did not block e2e.
Storybook build emits a chunk size warning from generated Storybook assets; it does not fail the build.
requested_action: Review the F005 test baseline, the lead scope reconciliations for root tooling files, the command evidence, and decide ACCEPT / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK.
